exhibitions.description: "I’m a remarkable woman – always was, though none of you seemed to think so."May Morris

Visitors to the Lady Lever Art Gallery can enjoy a rare opportunity to see conservation work in action on gallery. Thanks to funding from the Lord Leverhulme Charitable Trust, the gallery has been able to focus on the conservation of four Spanish altarpiece panels, dating from the early 1400s.

The Lady Lever Art Gallery opened in December 1922 and houses the fine and decorative art collection of its founder, William Hesketh Lever. Lever’s paintings, sculpture, furniture, ceramics and embroideries were made more than 100 years ago and are among the best in the UK. National Museums Liverpool employs people with specialist skills to look after these collections. Objects on display and in storage are at risk of damage. Damp, dust, insects, light, and wear and tear all affect their appearance.

exhibitions.exhibition_title: Innocence and Beauty: Children and Animals in Art (1700-1900)
exhibitions.description: Innocence and Beauty explores how artists between 1700 and 1900 reimagined childhood and its connection with the natural world.In earlier centuries, children were viewed as miniature adults, valued for their labour or social usefulness and expected to behave like their elders. By the 19th century, however, childhood came to be seen as a distinct and precious stage of life — a time of innocence, imagination, and emotional growth.

Featuring an inspiring range of 90 winning artworks created by pupils from primary, secondary and SEND schools across the Liverpool City Region and beyond, the exhibition will offer an insight into the quality of work of our young aspiring artists.Established in 2012, dot-art Schools is an interactive online inter-school art competition for children in Year 5 and Year 9 taking place annually and culminating in a prize-giving event and public exhibition of the winning artworks. The impact of the programme has grown each year, with more than 11,500 taking part so far.

exhibitions.exhibition_title: John Akomfrah: Listening All Night To The Rain
exhibitions.description: 'Listening All Night To The Rain' by British artist Sir John Akomfrah is a series of multi-channel video installations. It was commissioned for the British Pavilion at the 60th Venice Biennale in 2024.

What is gender? How have ideas about it changed over time? How does it shape our day-to-day lives? These questions and more will be explored in a brand-new exhibition co-created by National Museums Liverpool.Through a diverse collection of fine and decorative art, personal stories and objects, Gender Stories traces the spectrum of genders and their expression across time and place, examining how gender intersects with our sex, class, sexuality and heritage to shape who we are.

exhibitions.exhibition_title: All that Glitters: Lustre decoration on ceramics 1900-2025
exhibitions.description: Twelve beautiful pieces of art pottery decorated with gold lustre can be seen in Room 11. Potters in the Middle East were the first to embellish ceramics with lustre glazes over one thousand years ago. Inspired by these glittering wares, 20th-century potters rediscovered an ancient and difficult technique with dazzling effect. Renowned ceramicists Sir Edmund Elton, Alan Caiger Smith and Sutton Taylor are among the artists featured.

A captivating new display celebrating the groundbreaking fashion designer's remarkable career spanning over five decades. This stunning showcase brings together a collection of Westwood's revolutionary designs, from her punk beginnings to her status as a British fashion icon.

Join us at the Walker Art Gallery for a tour of Liverpool's most outstanding art collection and discover the masterpieces hidden within the National Gallery of the North.Discover the history of the gallery, from its opening in 1877, through its development into one of the most important galleries in the country.

Prepare to be captivated by a stunning new permanent display featuring over 200 magnificent artworks, including paintings, sculptures, decorative art objects, prints, and drawings. The galleries show newly acquired masterpieces, never before seen by the public, alongside the long-awaited return of beloved visitor favourites. Immerse yourself in the rich world of art in a dedicated space for the Walker's remarkable collection of works on paper, including exquisite watercolours, intricate drawings, and captivating prints.
Big Art for Little Artists is a dedicated children’s gallery within the Walker Art Gallery. It has been designed for children aged 0-5 years to enjoy with their grown-ups.

Forty years since the Merseyside Museums and Galleries Order was presented to Queen Elizabeth II (12 February 1986), we're inviting you to dig, dive and delve into our vast collections, to celebrate four decades of Liverpool’s national museum network.

exhibitions.exhibition_title: Minkisi, Slavery and Colonialism - An International Slavery Museum Intervention
exhibitions.description: A new intervention in World Museum by curators from International Slavery Museum explores the links between transatlantic slavery and colonial collections from 26 February.Set among a display of 19th century minkisi – figures or containers of spiritual significance from the Kongo region (in present day Angola and Democratic Republic of the Congo) – LR Vandy’s artwork, ‘Pomp & Circumstance’ (2019), creates space for a new dialogue on these deeply symbolic objects.

Cuerpos del Tiempo (Bodies of Time) is a collaborative project in partnership with Luma Creations, funded by the Esmée Fairbairn Collections Fund. This initiative will engage Latin American communities across the North West of England to help reshape the interpretation of the museum’s Latin American collections for display in 2025-2026.

Currents of Change photography exhibition: Lived Histories of Liverpool's Waterfront, is a community photography exhibition in which twelve Liverpool residents explore their lived relationships with the city's waterfront. Through their own photographs and words, participants tell stories of migration, labour, belonging, memory and environmental change along Liverpool's docks and shores.

exhibitions.exhibition_title: Reflections of Liverpool: The Howells’ Family Album
exhibitions.description: Bob Howells was a keen amateur photographer who built a makeshift darkroom inside the wardrobe of his Kensington home to produce wonderfully evocative images. On his death he left his grandson, Steven, two of his cameras and a tin containing film and glass plate negatives. Years passed and when Steven developed them gems from mid 20th century Liverpool life were revealed.

exhibitions.exhibition_title: Phantasmagoria: Folkloric Sculpture for the Digital Age
exhibitions.description: This major exhibition brings together a new generation of artists working across sculpture, moving image, performance, video games and installation to explore how digital technologies are reshaping what sculpture can be and how it can be used to tell stories about our past, present and future.

exhibitions.description: This display focuses on bookworks by Japanese artist Yoko Terauchi (b.1954), which explore interior and exterior as a continuous plane.

exhibitions.exhibition_title: Some Steel: Sculpture and Steel in Britain, 1960-90
exhibitions.description: This display traces the relationship between sculpture and steel over a period of thirty years, from the gallery to artist-run spaces, to the street and the outdoor museum, from metropolitan London to the post-industrial North.

exhibitions.exhibition_title: A History of Sculpture in Leeds
exhibitions.description: This timeline charts the history of both Leeds Art Gallery, which opened in 1888, the achievements of Henry Moore and the development of the Henry Moore Institute.
Sundaram's practice bridges material traditions and sculptural form, reimagining felt-making as an act of construction, layering and transformation.

See an original Monet masterpiece at Grundy Art Gallery this spring and experience a new sound artwork produced by Blackpool’s Young People’s Charity, The Magic Club in response to Monet’s work. This spring visitors will have the rare opportunity to fully experience Monet’s breathtaking The Petit Bras of the Seine at Argenteuil (1872) in person […]

exhibitions.exhibition_title: LOUISE GIOVANELLI: FROM HERE TO HERE TO HERE
exhibitions.description: In spring 2016 Louise Giovanelli had her very first solo exhibition at Grundy Art Gallery. The exhibition was titled From Here to Here and comprised of new paintings all made in response to works from The Grundy Art Gallery Collection. In the 10 years since that date, Giovanelli has become one of the most celebrated […]

Presented in conversation with, but separate to Louise Giovanelli’s solo exhibition From Here to Here to Here, this exhibition platforms emerging painters from the 2024 and 2025 cohort of Apollo Painting School. The exhibition assembles a range of approaches to painting that reflect its continued relevance as a critical and evolving medium. While points of […]

exhibitions.exhibition_title: Setting the Stage: 70 Years of the Eurovision Song Contest
Exhibition: Four Seasons In One Day
exhibitions.description: Immerse yourself in the changing seasons with our co-curated exhibition developed with the Action for Autism Barnsley group (AfAB).

exhibitions.exhibition_title: Barnsley’s Radical Collector: The Sadler Gift from Turner to Bloomsbury
exhibitions.description: The exhibition features works from renowned artists, such as Edward Wadsworth, Jacob Kramer, Vanessa Bell and many more.
The Straight A’s exhibition offers a deeply personal look into the adult life of Barnsley born artist, John Ledger, told through five powerful themes: Anxiety, Anorexia, Alcohol, Ageing, and Art.

exhibitions.exhibition_title: Exhibition: Light and Soul - Early Impressions of The French Landscape
exhibitions.description: The painters of Light and Soul drew inspiration from ancient forests and valleys, contemporary life in the landscape and the shifting light over rivers and seas. The exhibition brings together 17 exceptional artists including Pierre-Henri de Valenciennes, Camille Corot, Rosa Bonheur and Eugène Boudin.
An inspirational new mini-exhibition as part of a creative health programme designed to support vulnerable adults experiencing loneliness and isolation across Barnsley.

Discover the world of Studio Prints through a new exhibition celebrating the Balakjian Collection. Gifted to the Cooper Gallery through Arts Council England’s Cultural Gifts Scheme, the newly acquired group of prints, plates, and drawings offer a glimpse into the art of printmaking.

Discover Post, a new exhibition from artist Benny Watson exploring themes of identity, loss and renewal. Created in the year following the death of his grandmother, Mel Dyke MBE, Watson uses old postcards and materials found in Mel's home to create bold new works.

Take inspiration from the Studio Prints exhibition in this relaxed, beginner-friendly workshop. Learn the basics of linocut, from designing and transferring your image to carving and hand-printing using traditional techniques.
Whether you're a seasoned sketcher or picking up a pencil for the very first time, this relaxed and welcoming session invites you to explore the calming, mindful benefits of drawing.
Relaxed Openings at Barnsley Museums

exhibitions.exhibition_title: The Creative at Cooper Craft Club: Luscious Landscapes
exhibitions.description: Highlighting our four new landscapes on display, you will have a chance to create your own 3D pop up tunnel picture, design a picture postcard using collage techniques and learn some tricks and techniques to for perspective drawing.
Whether you're a seasoned sketcher or picking up a pencil for the very first time, this relaxed and welcoming session invites you to explore the calming, mindful benefits of drawing.
Come along to our relaxed openings, which are designed for visitors who would prefer a quieter experience in the museum.
Discover the Vivienne Westwood: Rebel Storyteller & Visionary exhibition at The Bowes Museum — a bold exploration of the design icon’s pioneering fashion legacy.
Fletcher explores the transformative power of clothing and examines the idea that we dress to manifest different versions of ourselves.
Explore the Founders’ Galleries at The Bowes Museum, reimagining the story of Joséphine and John Bowes through new research, objects and interactive displays.
Challenge assumptions, look beyond tradition and explore fresh perspectives on the nude form in this co-produced display at The Bowes Museum.

exhibitions.exhibition_title: Nathan Coley: You Create What You Will and Tate Modern on Fire
exhibitions.description: An illuminated outdoor installation by Turner prize nominee, Nathan Coley (b.1972), sited in the grounds of The Bowes Museum, Barnard Castle. Towering 5.5 metres tall, You Create What You Will invites the viewer to reflect on the profound power of creation.

exhibitions.exhibition_title: Norman Cornish: A Life in Sketchbooks
exhibitions.description: Discover Norman Cornish: A Life in Sketchbooks at The Bowes Museum, exploring the artist’s creative process through rarely seen drawings, journals and personal works.

exhibitions.exhibition_title: Impressions of Light: Masterpieces from MuMa – Le Havre and The Bowes Museum
exhibitions.description: Experience Impressionism at The Bowes Museum in ‘Impressions of Light’ — masterpieces from MUMA Le Havre and The Bowes Museum illuminating the art of light and colour.

exhibitions.exhibition_title: Lace and Legacy: Fashion Treasures from The Blackborne Collection
exhibitions.description: Explore the Blackborne Lace Collection at The Bowes Museum in the Lace & Legacy exhibition, showcasing exquisite historic lace and fashion treasures.

exhibitions.exhibition_title: To See and Be Seen by Conor Collins
exhibitions.description: Join us at HOME to attend the preview for Conor Collins' exhibition - To See and Be Seen - coinciding with the launch of Queer Up North 1992 - 2002.
One of the largest contemporary art galleries in the UK, presenting free exhibitions, special events, and family activities.

exhibitions.description: One of the largest contemporary art galleries in the UK, presenting free exhibitions, special events, and family activities.

Abbot Hall is a Grade I listed Georgian building that has been part of Kendal's history for over 250 years. It opened as a gallery in 1962.

Enjoy a light lunch, sandwich or tempting treat at Windermere Jetty Museum’s lakeshore café. With extraordinary views over the lake, it is the perfect place to relax and grab a bite to eat. Family and dog friendly.
We hope you enjoy your visit to Abbot Hall. There is ground floor access and lifts to upper gallery levels, a creative space and coffee bar.
